pattern: A stack carrying each character with its current run length collapses any run that reaches k, handling cascading removals in a single linear pass.
hints: Track how many times the current character has repeated in a row as you scan left to right.
A stack of (character, runLength) lets a new match increment the top, and a full run of k pops it.
Push (char, 1) or increment the top's count when it matches; when the count hits k pop it, then rebuild the string by repeating each surviving character its stored number of times.

edge_inputs.description: nothing to remove when no run reaches k
removals cascade, exposing left and right neighbors that then combine
exactly k identical characters fully cancel
one over k leaves a single character behind
interleaved runs require cascade after the inner run clears

stress.description: long random string over a tiny alphabet to force frequent cascading removals
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randrange(2, 5)
    return [s, k]
stress.note: small alphabet makes runs of length k common
